message:
fix: prevent integer overflow in Duration-to-u64 conversions and HPACK bitmask shifts

Three independent overflow/truncation bugs fixed across the codebase:

circuit_breaker: SlidingWindow::cleanup() cast Duration::as_millis() (u128) to
u64 via `as u64`, silently truncating durations longer than ~585 years. Replaced
with u64::try_from().unwrap_or(u64::MAX) so the window_start saturates instead
of wrapping. Added test proving Duration::MAX no longer over-evicts entries.

supervision: Seven call sites converted `duration.as_nanos() as u64` which
truncates identically. Extracted a `duration_nanos_u64()` helper using the same
try_from/saturate pattern. Affects RestartHistory::can_restart,
record_restart, recent_restart_count, RestartIntensityWindow::record, intensity,
count, and the hash_restart_config / hash_backoff_strategy deterministic hashing
functions. Added tests confirming Duration::MAX windows retain all entries.

hpack: Huffman encode/decode used `(1u64 << bits) - 1` to build bitmasks, which
is undefined behavior when bits == 64 (shift equals or exceeds type width).
Replaced with a const-evaluated BIT_MASKS lookup table of 65 entries (0..=64)
computed at compile time, eliminating the shift entirely at runtime.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
